subject: abstract data types

subject: object
evidence: JVM implements HashMap as an array of HashMap$Entry objects with each object having 4 fields: 1. int hash value computed using a hash function 2. Object key 3. Object value 4. Object next reference to the next HashMap$Entry object as the HashMap is implemented as a linked list

subject: linkedlist

subject: linkedlist

subject: default constructor
evidence: 1. Default constructor LinkedList() creates an empty linked list

subject: linkedlist

subject: arraylist
evidence: ArrayList class is an implementation of a dynamic array data structure where the size of the array automatically changes when items are dynamically added (or removed)

subject: integer

subject: arraylist
evidence: If the structure runs out of space when attempting to insert a new item, a bigger-sized structure is created by allocating new memory from the heap with current memory contents copied to the newly allocated space
